assembly_instructions: Read GLM-5.1 as the LIKELY next step beyond GLM-4.5: same deep-narrow MoE shape, but PUSHED. 128 layers (+33%) climb the violet residual pillar. Each block has GQA-with-MLA-compressed-KV attention on the left (96 query heads sharing 8 KV heads, but with a learned KV down-projection that adopts DeepSeek's MLA trick on top of GQA), and a fine-grained MoE FFN on the right (1 shared + 256 routed experts, top-8) — note 256 experts vs GLM-4.5's 160. The orange ring at X=6 is the routed expert bank with the same group-limited dispatch (≤4 of 8 device groups) plus a NEW expert-overflow shared expert. The amber column at X=-3.5 is the compressed KV cache. The bronze cube at X=2.5/Y=8 is a depth-2 MTP head (was depth-1 in GLM-4.5) for stronger speculative decoding. The titanium slab on the far left is the Muon optimiser — adopted from DeepSeek-V4 for matrix params, with AdamW kept for embeddings/head/RMSNorm. Click any part for details. THIS IS A FORWARD-LOOKING PROJECTION — numbers should be read as plausible extrapolation, not fact.

info.summary: PROJECTED next-generation flagship from Zhipu / Z.ai, extrapolated from GLM-4.5's published research direction. No public paper at scene time. Best guess: ~600B total / ~48B activated, deep-narrow at 128 × 5760, GQA(96/8) augmented with an MLA-style compressed KV down-projection, 1 shared + 256 routed experts (top-8) with group-limited dispatch, depth-2 MTP for stronger speculative decoding, Muon optimiser adopted for the matrix bulk, FP4 QAT on routed experts, and 256K native context. Treat numbers as plausible extrapolation, not fact.
info.description:
GLM-5.1 is presented here as a research-trajectory PROJECTION, not a paper. Where GLM-4.5 went deep-narrow with 96 layers / 5120 hidden / 160 experts top-8 + 1 shared + group-limited routing, GLM-5.1 likely (i) adds another ~33% depth (128 layers) and a small width bump (5760), (ii) doubles the routed-expert count (160 → 256) while keeping top-8 active, (iii) adopts an MLA-style learned KV compression on top of GQA — a known cross-pollination trick from DeepSeek-V3 — to halve KV memory at long context, (iv) raises MTP depth from 1 to 2 (small quality + speculative-decoding wins), (v) extends native context from 128K to 256K via training rather than YaRN, (vi) crosses over to the Muon optimiser for the matrix bulk after its DeepSeek-V4 success, while keeping AdamW for the carve-out (embed / head / RMSNorm / mHC scalars / router bias), and (vii) brings FP4 QAT to routed experts.

Design rationale, in one line each:
• Deeper-narrower still: more layers > wider hidden for reasoning at fixed FLOPs.
• 256 fine-grained experts: doubles capacity without changing the active-FLOPs budget.
• MLA on top of GQA: stacks two KV-shrinking tricks for cheap 256K context.
• Depth-2 MTP: ~2.4× speculative speedup vs depth-1.
• Muon adoption: ~5–10% wall-clock saving at this scale per the V4 results.
• FP4 experts: ~4× expert-weight memory shrink vs BF16.

comparisons.delta: Same family. Projected GLM-5.1 grows: total params 355B → 600B, activated 32B → 48B (~50% larger compute / token), depth 96 → 128 (deeper), width 5120 → 5760 (slightly wider), routed experts 160 → 256, KV path GQA → GQA+MLA, MTP depth 1 → 2, optimiser AdamW-only → Muon+AdamW, native ctx 32K (trained) / 128K (YaRN) → 256K (trained). Routing scheme (sigmoid loss-free + group-limited ≤4 of 8) is preserved unchanged because the V4.5 design study showed it scales well.

Different bets. V4-Pro pushes attention sparsity (CSA+HCA hybrid + lightning indexer) and has 384 routed experts top-6. GLM-5.1 stays dense-attention (GQA+MLA) but doubles down on depth and expert count. Activated params per token are similar (~48B vs ~49B), but V4-Pro has ~3× more total capacity and natively supports 1M context vs GLM-5.1's projected 256K. GLM keeps the deep-narrow doctrine; V4 stays moderately wide (7168) and very deep (61).

GLM-5.1 is the most CONSERVATIVE projection — it does not add linear / hybrid attention. Qwen3.6 likely adopts Gated DeltaNet (linear) for 75% of layers; MiniMax-M2.7 likely pushes the lightning:softmax ratio further. GLM stays with full softmax + MLA-compressed KV, betting that depth + expert count + a small KV trick beats hybrid attention at the 256K context point.

Public consensus on GPT-5-class peers is unstable. Versus the most-rumoured shape (large dense or coarse-MoE with full softmax + GQA + FP8), GLM-5.1 is much sparser per token (48B activated of 600B), uses an extra MLA layer on top of GQA (smaller KV), and is open-weight. Activated FLOPs / token in projected GLM-5.1 are far below a comparably-capable closed dense model — the cost story is the entire pitch.

parts.name: Input Tokens
parts.role: Token-ID stream from the multilingual BPE tokeniser.

parts.name: Embedding (V≈152K, d=5760)
parts.role: Tied embedding / LM head. Trained by AdamW (in the Muon carve-out).

parts.name: Residual Stream (d=5760, 128 layers)
parts.role: Pre-norm RMSNorm residual carry through 128 layers.

parts.name: × 128 Layers (deeper than GLM-4.5)
parts.role: Marker for the projected 128-layer depth (+33% vs GLM-4.5). Only 7 are rendered.

parts.name: Block 1
parts.role: First Transformer block.

parts.name: Block 2
parts.role: Block 2.

parts.name: Block 3 (exploded right at X=2)
parts.role: Block 3 — sub-components shown to the left and right.

parts.name: Block 4
parts.role: Block 4.

parts.name: Block 5
parts.role: Block 5.

parts.name: Block 127
parts.role: Block 127. (Layers 6–126 elided.)

parts.name: Block 128 (final)
parts.role: Final Transformer block.

parts.name: Attention · Pre-RMSNorm
parts.role: Pre-norm RMSNorm.

parts.name: GQA + MLA · QKV Proj (96 Q / 8 KV / c_kv≈768)
parts.role: Combined trick: GQA-shape (96 Q / 8 KV) on the surface, but K and V are computed via a LOW-RANK latent c_kv ≈ 768. Cache stores only the latent, decoder reconstructs full KV. Stacks two KV-shrinking ideas.
parts.memory_footprint: Cache: c_kv × FP8 ≈ 768 B / token / layer (~2× smaller than plain GQA(8))
parts.algorithm:
Q       = x @ W_Q                  # full 96-head projection
kv_lat  = x @ W_DKV                # [B,T, c_kv=768]   ← cached
K_full  = kv_lat @ W_UK            # reconstruct on demand
V_full  = kv_lat @ W_UV
Q, K_full = qk_rmsnorm(Q, K_full)
Q, K_full = apply_partial_rope(Q, K_full)

parts.name: QK-Norm + Partial RoPE
parts.role: RMSNorm on Q & K + partial-RoPE (half head dims rotated). Inherited from GLM-4.5.

parts.name: FlashAttention-3 (causal, 256K)
parts.role: Standard causal attention; uses FlashAttention-3 with FP8 GEMMs on Hopper/Blackwell.

parts.name: Attention · O-Projection
parts.role: Output projection back to d=5760.

parts.name: MLA-Compressed KV Cache (latent, FP8)
parts.role: Stores only the c_kv ≈ 768-dim latent per token per layer; full K/V reconstructed on read. ~2× smaller than GQA(8) at the same head count.
parts.memory_footprint: ≈ 768 B × FP8 / token / layer × 128 layers × 256K ctx ≈ 25 GiB / sequence
improvement_ideas: INT4 KV with per-channel scales for ~2× more context.
Page cold prefixes to NVMe for shared system prompts.

parts.name: FFN · Pre-Norm RMSNorm
parts.role: Pre-norm before the MoE FFN.

parts.name: Top-8 Sigmoid Router (loss-free balance)
parts.role: Per-token affinity = sigmoid(x @ W_r) + b_balance. Top-8 of 256 routed experts. Bias-update load balancing inherited from GLM-4.5.
parts.algorithm:
scores = sigmoid(x @ W_router) + b_balance
topk_idx, topk_w = topk(scores, 8)
topk_w = topk_w / topk_w.sum()
# group-limited: at most 4 of 8 device groups

parts.name: Group-Limited Dispatch (≤4 of 8)
parts.role: Constraint that caps cross-node all-to-all bandwidth. Inherited from GLM-4.5.

parts.name: Expert All-to-All
parts.role: NCCL all-to-all dispatch + combine, kept small by the group limit.

parts.name: Routed Experts (256, top-8)
parts.role: Per-MoE-layer bank of 256 fine-grained SwiGLU experts (d_ff ≈ 1408). 8 active per token. FP4 QAT weights.
parts.memory_footprint: 256 × 3·d·d_ff × FP4 ≈ 6 GiB / layer; 128 layers ≈ 0.77 TiB of routed FFN weights
improvement_ideas: Group GEMM to fuse 8 expert calls.
Mixed FP4/FP6 for the most loss-sensitive experts.

parts.name: Shared Expert (×1)
parts.role: Always-on expert that absorbs common computation and serves as overflow when capacity is hit.

parts.name: FP4 Expert Weights (QAT)
parts.role: Quantisation-aware training disk under the routed-expert bank. ~4× memory shrink vs BF16.

parts.name: LM Head (tied)
parts.role: Tied projection back to ~152K vocab. Trained by AdamW (in the Muon carve-out).

parts.name: MTP Head (depth=2, projected)
parts.role: Projection: GLM-5.1 likely raises MTP depth from 1 to 2. Predicts t+1 AND t+2 jointly. Speculative-decoding speedup ~2.4× (was ~1.8× with depth-1).
parts.algorithm:
for k in 1..2:
    h_k     = mtp_block_k(h_final)
    logits_k = h_k @ E.T
    loss   += w_k * CE(logits_k, target_t+k)

parts.name: LM Loss
parts.role: Primary next-token cross-entropy.

parts.name: MTP Loss (depth-2 weighted)
parts.role: Auxiliary depth-2 MTP loss with decreasing weights for deeper steps.

parts.name: Mode: 'think'
parts.role: Reasoning mode (system-prompt switched).

parts.name: Mode: 'no-think'
parts.role: Direct-answer mode.

parts.name: Muon (matrix bulk) — adopted from V4
parts.role: PROJECTED adoption of Muon for the matrix bulk after DeepSeek-V4's wins. Newton-Schulz orthonormalised updates, Nesterov 0.95.
parts.algorithm:
M = β·M + G
U = newton_schulz(M, iters=10)
W -= lr * (rms_scale * U + wd * W)

parts.name: AdamW (carve-out)
parts.role: Carve-out for embedding, prediction head, RMSNorm γ, router bias, attention sink, mHC scalars (if any).

parts.name: Parallelism Plan: TP × EP × PP × DP
parts.role: Multi-axis parallelism plan. Group-limited routing keeps EP all-to-all bounded across 8 device groups.

parts.name: ~30T-token Corpus + Long-CoT RL
parts.role: Projected pre-training corpus + reasoning RL phase.
